book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II

title: Of Dyeing the Hair.

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: I see some (women) turn (the colour of) their hair with saffron.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: They are ashamed even of their own nation, (ashamed) that their procreation did not assign them to Germany and to Gaul: thus, as it is, they transfer their hair (thither)!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Ill, ay, most ill, do they augur for themselves with their flame-coloured head, and think that graceful which (in fact) they are polluting!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Nay, moreover, the force of the cosmetics burns ruin into the hair; and the constant application of even any undrugged moisture, lays up a store of harm for the head; while the sun’s warmth, too, so desirable for imparting to the hair at once growth and dryness, is hurtful.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
On the Apparel of Women - Book II
On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Shall a Christian woman heap saffron on her head, as upon an altar?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For, whatever is wont to be burned to the honour of the unclean spirit, that—unless it is applied for honest, and necessary, and salutary uses, for which God’s creature was provided—may seem to be a sacrifice.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: But, however, God saith, “Which of you can make a white hair black, or out of a black a white?”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: And so they refute the Lord!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: “Behold!” say they, “instead of white or black, we make it yellow,—more winning in grace.”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: And yet such as repent of having lived to old age do attempt to change it even from white to black!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: The age which is the object of our wishes and prayers blushes (for itself)! a theft is effected! youth, wherein we have sinned, is sighed after! the opportunity of sobriety is spoiled!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Far from Wisdom’s daughters be folly so great!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: The more old age tries to conceal itself, the more will it be detected.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Here is a veritable eternity, in the (perennial) youth of your head!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Here we have an “incorruptibility” to “put on,” with a view to the new house of the Lord which the divine monarchy promises!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Well do you speed toward the Lord; well do you hasten to be quit of this most iniquitous world, to whom it is unsightly to approach (your own) end!
